package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for url_parse_in_loop_on_invariant_base: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: url.Parse(...) or ParseRequestURI(...) observed inside loops with a stable-looking base input.
type CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseModel
}

type CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseService struct {
    repo *CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBase(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseUrlParseInLoopOnInvariantBaseDTO) error {
    focus := "url_parse_in_loop_on"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    for _, item := range items { _ = strings.ToLower(item.ID); time.Now() }
    url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive := map[string]int{"url": 1, "parse": 2, "loop": 3, "invariant": 4, "base": 5}
    url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    url_positive_0 := url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive["url"] + len(input)
    _ = url_positive_0
    parse_positive_1 := url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive["parse"] + len(input)
    _ = parse_positive_1
    for loop_positive_2Index, item := range items { url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive[item.ID] = loop_positive_2Index }
    invariant_positive_3 := url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive["invariant"] + len(input)
    _ = invariant_positive_3
    base_positive_4 := url_parse_loop_invariant_base_positive["base"] + len(input)
    _ = base_positive_4
    return nil
}
